task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the L4-L5 vertebral level on the abdominal CT scan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial slice displayed in the medical imaging software corresponds to the L4-L5 intervertebral disc space or the L4/L5 vertebral bodies.

task_completion.description: The agent segmented the subcutaneous (SAT) and visceral (VAT) adipose tissue compartments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Colored overlays or masks representing SAT and VAT are visible on the axial CT slice, typically isolated using a specific threshold range.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the fat compartment segmentation to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file explorer showing 'fat_segmentation.nii.gz' being saved to or existing in '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/'.

task_completion.description: The agent created a JSON report containing all the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal showing a JSON structure with the exact keys: 'measurement_level', 'slice_index', 'sat_area_cm2', 'vat_area_cm2', 'vat_sat_ratio', and 'fat_distribution'.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the completed fat analysis report with calculated values to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'fat_analysis_report.json' is saved in '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' and contains populated numerical values for the areas and ratio, and a classification string.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a medical imaging application to perform the segmentation and measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the UI of an imaging software (e.g., 3D Slicer) with the CT scan loaded, rather than just a text editor or a script outputting dummy files.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed measurements or thresholding in the software before creating the JSON report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The imaging software shows thresholding tools (using the -190 to -30 HU range), segment statistics, or area measurement tools being actively used on the fat compartments.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the JSON values without deriving them from the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values entered into the JSON report match the output of the segment statistics or measurement tools visible in the imaging software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The standard Hounsfield Unit (HU) range for quantifying adipose tissue (both subcutaneous and visceral) in CT scans is -190 to -30. The L4-L5 vertebral level is the recommended clinical standard for visceral fat quantification.
